items.title: Boxes.dev: Run Claude Code and Codex in the cloud instead of localhost
items.why_it_matters: A new cloud-only environment launched that lets you run Claude Code and Codex in the cloud instead of your local machine. This matters because you can run long-running agent tasks without keeping your laptop open or draining your battery. Try signing up for boxes.dev this week and run a Claude Code session on your AI-ingest repo from the cloud.

items.title: Cost.dev: Make your coding agents cost-aware and cheaper to run
items.why_it_matters: A new CLI tool launched to help developers monitor and reduce the API costs generated by coding agents. This matters because running agents like Claude Code can quickly rack up token costs if left unchecked. Try running the cost.dev CLI against your current project this week to see where your agent prompts are costing you the most.

items.title: Chatcode: Remote control for Claude Code and Codex via Telegram
items.why_it_matters: A new tool launched that lets you remotely control your Claude Code and Codex sessions via a web interface or Telegram. This matters because it allows you to check on, pause, or prompt your coding agents from your phone while away from your desk. Try installing the Chatcode gateway this week and send a prompt to your local setup via Telegram.

items.title: Open Code Review: AI-powered code reviews in your CLI
items.why_it_matters: An open-source CLI tool has been released to automatically review your code changes using AI before you commit them. This matters because you are currently learning Git discipline and need to verify the code your AI agents write. Try running this tool on your next AI-ingest branch this week to get an automated critique before you push to GitHub.

items.title: Resume: Manage Claude and Codex sessions in one CLI
items.why_it_matters: A new CLI tool launched to help you manage, pause, and resume your Claude and Codex terminal sessions. This matters because it keeps your terminal organized and lets you jump back into previous coding contexts without losing your history. Try installing resume this week to manage your different AI-ingest feature branches.
